resources.string: If you use a cane, walker, rollator, or crutches, use it as you normally do. The pipeline measures the rhythm of your walking, and trends within your own device, using the same aid each time, are what matter. Absolute stride length values are calibrated for unaided walking and may read low when an aid is used.

Hold your phone with both hands. Tap the left and right circles, alternating, as fast as you can manage. Tap with the hand you usually write or text with first, then the other hand. Each round lasts 30 seconds.
You will do two 30 second rounds. Tap with the hand you usually write or text with first, then the other hand.
Rest for a moment, then we will start the other hand.

You will read a short familiar passage aloud for 30 seconds. Hold the phone at a comfortable distance and read in your natural speaking voice. If you stumble on a word, keep going. The test ends automatically after 30 seconds.

This is a smartphone variant of a clinical read aloud task. The numbers it produces are for personal tracking on this device and are not directly comparable to a clinician administered voice assessment.
The microphone records only while you read. The recording is processed on your phone and never leaves the device.
Voice test unavailable on this device
Microphone access is required for this test. Voice test is off for now.
Some background noise. Recording will still work.
Find a quieter room and we will re check the level.

You wish to know all about my grandfather. Well, he is nearly ninety three years old, yet he still thinks as swiftly as ever. He dresses himself in an ancient black frock coat, usually several buttons missing. A long beard clings to his chin, giving those who observe him a pronounced feeling of the utmost respect. When he speaks, his voice is just a bit cracked and quivers a trifle. Twice each day he plays skillfully and with zest upon a small organ. Except in the winter when the snow or ice prevents, he slowly takes a short walk in the open air each day. We have often urged him to walk more and smoke less, but he always answers, “Banana oil!” Grandfather likes to be modern in his language.
You read the passage for %1$d seconds. Your voice was steady most of the time.
You read the passage for %1$d seconds. Your voice had some variation.
You read the passage for %1$d seconds. Your voice varied more than usual today.
You read the passage for %1$d seconds.
Voice patterns change over time. These numbers will be most useful when compared against your own past tests on this phone, not against anyone else’s results.
Save audio of your voice tests for personal review
When on, your voice recordings are saved on this phone so you can listen back to them yourself. They never leave your device. When off, only the test results are saved, not the recordings.
Set a lock screen on this phone to enable audio retention.
Save audio of your voice tests?
Audio is saved on this phone, never leaves your device. You can turn this off any time, and existing recordings will be deleted.
Turn off recording retention? Existing recordings will be deleted from this phone immediately.
These trends are personal to you. They will vary day to day. Look at the longer pattern.
You have not run a check in yet. The trends here will fill in after your first session.
No sessions recorded for this test yet. Run a check in to start seeing your trends.
%1$d sessions recorded; trend below shows latest %2$d.
Run another check in to see how this changes over time.
Personal trends only. Not a clinical assessment.
Less reliable capture. Variability is normal; longer trends matter more than single sessions.
Short capture. Try the full duration next time.
Noisy capture. The data is kept; try a quieter room next time.
Short capture. Fewer than 10 valid taps; the data is kept.
Short capture. Fewer than 20 detected steps; the data is kept.

The Trends screens show your own values over time. They are personal to you. This app does not compare your values against clinical norms or population averages in this version; trends here are personal trends only.

The quality band on each session (Reliable, Mostly reliable, Less reliable) reflects how cleanly the recording was captured, not how well you did. Less reliable sessions still count; longer trends matter more than single sessions.
All of your data stays on this device. Nothing is sent to a cloud or to anyone else unless you choose to export a report and share it yourself.
This is not a medical device. It does not diagnose or treat any condition. Do not change your treatment based on these results. Share with your neurologist for clinical decisions.
Stride length values are calibrated for within device trend comparison only; they are not directly comparable to clinical gait laboratory measurements.
Letters correct out of 40 (100 percent contrast)
Letters correct out of 40 (2.5 percent contrast)
Letters correct out of 40 (1.25 percent contrast)
Response time per symbol (seconds, standard deviation)
Errors as a fraction of attempts
Voice cycle to cycle frequency variation
Voice cycle to cycle amplitude variation
Voice pitch variability across the passage
Pause time as a fraction of the recording
Voice was too loud. Try a normal speaking volume next time.
Background was noisy. Try a quieter room next time.
Reading was too short. Try reading the full passage next time.
Pitch variation measurement was unreliable this session.
Amplitude variation measurement was unreliable this session.
Voice clarity measurement was unreliable this session.
Pitch variability measurement was unreliable this session.
Pause measurement was unreliable this session.
Your latest %1$s: %2$s. Past %3$d sessions: median %4$s. Range: %5$s to %6$s.
Your latest %1$s: %2$s. Last 4 sessions: median %3$s. Range: %4$s to %5$s.
voice cycle to cycle frequency variation
voice cycle to cycle amplitude variation
Healthy adult average plus or minus one SD (Givon 2009, n equals 25)

Personal trends are the main signal here. The reference band shows healthy adult averages from one published study; it does not represent a clinical assessment. The band reflects measurement variability on the Givon 2009 walking test, not on this phone in your pocket. Your own values may scatter wider.
Could not prepare your report. Please try again.
Not enough storage space to prepare your report.
This is a personal record. It is not a medical document. Share it with your neurologist for context when you see them next.
